payload.message:
Hey Leonardo — I built a CODEBASE_MAP.md for Cerebro using a new cartographer skill. It maps the entire repo: architecture, every route/screen, data flows, DB schema, AI services, and a navigation guide ('I want to do X → look at Y').

**Purpose:** Give you (and any sub-agents you spawn) instant context on the full codebase before making changes. Instead of exploring and guessing at structure, you inject this as context and start working immediately. Think of it as AGENTS.md but for the code itself.

**What to do with it:**
1. Review it for accuracy — you know the codebase better than me
2. Fix anything wrong (especially around dead code flags — I flagged claude.ts/gemini.ts/grok.ts as possibly unused)
3. Commit it to the Cerebro repo at `docs/CODEBASE_MAP.md`
4. Keep it updated — re-run the mapping after major changes (or I can set up a periodic refresh)

**The file is at:** `/data/workspace/projects/cerebro-map/CODEBASE_MAP.md`

You can also pull it from the workspace via Syncthing, or I can push it to a PR on the Cerebro repo if you prefer.

This is yours to own going forward — I just built the first version.
